name: Caraco Pharmaceutical Laboratories, Ltd. v. Novo Nordisk A/S
question:
(1) Does the counterclaim provision of the Hatch-Waxman Act only apply if Novo's Prandin patent does not claim any approved method of use?

(2) Does Novo's use code for Prandin qualify as "patent information submitted under subsections (b) and (c)" for the purposes of the act?
description: A case in which the Court held that counterclaims that challenge unpatented uses of generic drugs, even if other approved uses are still under patent, are allowable under the Hatch-Waxman Act.
